checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=7,260,000 (2009-12-31) growing to 25,463,000 (2012-12-31), a ~251% increase, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew from 370,340,000 (FY2009) to 620,255,000 (FY2012), a ~67% increase

Goodwill=103,070,000 (2010-12-31) jumped to 159,349,000 (2011-12-31), a 54.6% increase in a single year versus revenue growth of only 13.0% (SalesRevenueGoodsNet 540,506,000 FY2011 vs 478,237,000 FY2010)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet grew from 90,191,000 (2009-12-31) to 126,335,000 (2012-12-31), +40%, slower than SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of +67% (370,340,000 FY2009 to 620,255,000 FY2012)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly swings from 61,932,000 (Q4 2010) to -67,552,000 (Q3 2011) to -10,036,000 (Q4 2011) to 2,354,000 (Q3 2012) to -2,746,000 (Q4 2012) show substantial volatility, not smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent declined from 4,163,000 (2009-12-31, ~7.1% of AR) to 2,573,000 (2010-12-31, ~3.5% of AR) to 2,780,000 (2012-12-31, ~3.1% of AR) even as AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 58,462,000 to 88,958,000 over the same period

AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent fell from 25,989,000 (2010-12-31) to 20,880,000 (2012-12-31), a 19.7% decline, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet rose from 478,237,000 (FY2010) to 620,255,000 (FY2012), a 29.7% increase
AccountsPayableCurrent fell from 11,294,000 (2011-12-31) to 9,040,000 (2012-12-31), a 20% decline, while CostOfGoodsSold rose from 112,111,000 (FY2011) to 153,409,000 (FY2012), a 36.8% increase

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2010-05-26, only three months after the original 10-K filed 2010-02-26
8-K/A filings on 2008-10-09, 2009-07-23, and 2010-10-29 indicate repeated amendment of current reports

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans FY2007-Q1 2013 with annual and quarterly figures for revenue, net income, operating/investing/financing cash flows, receivables, inventory, goodwill, intangibles, and current liabilities, sourced across multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings (e.g., accessions 0000950123-11-018857, 0001193125-12-078937, 0001445305-13-000348, 0001142596-13-000004)
Some concepts (AccountsPayableCurrent, AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent) are only reported annually, limiting quarter-level granularity

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reserve/liability under-accrual: allowance for doubtful accounts fell as a percentage of receivables (from ~7.1% in 2009 to ~3.1% in 2012) and accrued liabilities/accounts payable declined in absolute terms even as revenue and COGS grew substantially, which could indicate reserve releases or under-accrual of expenses/liabilities that flatter reported net income and equity.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=25,989,000 (2010-12-31) declining to 20,880,000 (2012-12-31) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet rose from 478,237,000 (FY2010) to 620,255,000 (FY2012)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large non-operating/one-time items (e.g., a litigation settlement gain boosting Q4 2010 net income, and acquisition/contingent-consideration or non-cash convertible-debt interest charges depressing 2011-2012 net income) create a persistent, growing wedge between reported net income and operating cash flow, consistent with earnings being shaped by non-cash or non-recurring items rather than core operations.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly = 61,932,000 (Q4 2010) versus 1,088,000/6,723,000/8,542,000 in prior 2010 quarters, and NetIncomeLoss=-67,552,000 (Q3 2011)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Rapid, acquisition-driven capitalization of goodwill (+54.6% in 2011) and other non-current 'soft' assets (+251% from 2009 to 2012) funded partly by a large 2011 debt/equity raise (financing inflow of 209,879,000 in FY2011) could embed inflated purchase-accounting valuations, capitalized transaction/debt-issuance costs, or contingent consideration that may require future write-down, overstating the asset base relative to organic business growth.

top_signals: Declining allowance-for-doubtful-accounts coverage ratio (from ~7.1% to ~3.1% of AR) despite growing receivables, suggesting possible reserve release/understatement
Persistent and widening divergence between net income and operating cash flow (notably FY2012: NI=3,144,000 vs OCF=130,082,000), driven by large one-time/non-cash items

Rapid growth in 'soft' non-current assets (Goodwill +54.6% in 2011; Other Assets Noncurrent +251% 2009-2012) outpacing revenue growth (+67% over same span), tied to acquisitions and a large 2011 capital raise
Accrued liabilities and accounts payable declining in absolute terms while revenue and COGS grew materially, reducing expense/liability recognition relative to business scale
Filing chronology irregularities including a 10-K/A shortly after the original 10-K and an unusually dense cluster of 8-Ks in H2 2012
